title: When the length of a SystemVerilog array parameter (A[B]) is another parameter(B), verilator always use default value of B
body:
**module bti_demux:**

```systemverilog
module bti_demux #(
    parameter BTI_AW = 32,
    parameter BTI_DW = 32,
    parameter GST_SEL_AW = 8,
    parameter GST_NUM = 4,
    parameter logic [GST_SEL_AW-1:0] GST_SEL[GST_NUM],
    parameter int GST_AW[GST_NUM]
) // ...
```
the default value of `GST_NUM` is 4.

When instancing `bti_demux` with GST_NUM as 2:

```systemverilog
    localparam I_BTI_GST_NUM = 2;
    // ...
    bti_demux #(
        .BTI_AW           (`RV_AW),
        .BTI_DW           (`RV_XLEN),
        .GST_SEL_AW       (BTI_GST_SEL_AW),
        .GST_NUM          (I_BTI_GST_NUM),
        .GST_SEL          ('{BOOT_ROM_SEL, ITCM_SEL}),
        .GST_AW           ('{BOOT_ROM_AW, ITCM_AW})
    ) // ...
```
I get the following compiling error:
```text
%Error: /home/mfeng/repos/rv32i/rtl/soc.sv:123:28: Assignment pattern missed initializing elements: 2
                                                 : ... In instance sim_top.u_soc
  123 |         .GST_SEL          ('{BOOT_ROM_SEL, ITCM_SEL}),
      |                            ^~
        /home/mfeng/repos/rv32i/rtl/soc.sv:2:1: ... note: In file included from soc.sv
%Error: /home/mfeng/repos/rv32i/rtl/soc.sv:123:28: Assignment pattern missed initializing elements: 3
                                                 : ... In instance sim_top.u_soc
  123 |         .GST_SEL          ('{BOOT_ROM_SEL, ITCM_SEL}),
      |                            ^~
        /home/mfeng/repos/rv32i/rtl/soc.sv:2:1: ... note: In file included from soc.sv
%Error: /home/mfeng/repos/rv32i/rtl/soc.sv:124:28: Assignment pattern missed initializing elements: 2
                                                 : ... In instance sim_top.u_soc
  124 |         .GST_AW           ('{BOOT_ROM_AW, ITCM_AW})
      |                            ^~
        /home/mfeng/repos/rv32i/rtl/soc.sv:2:1: ... note: In file included from soc.sv
%Error: /home/mfeng/repos/rv32i/rtl/soc.sv:124:28: Assignment pattern missed initializing elements: 3
                                                 : ... In instance sim_top.u_soc
  124 |         .GST_AW           ('{BOOT_ROM_AW, ITCM_AW})
      |                            ^~
        /home/mfeng/repos/rv32i/rtl/soc.sv:2:1: ... note: In file included from soc.sv
%Error: Exiting due to 4 error(s)
```

Both Synopsys VCS and Xilinx Vivado work fine.

If I fill 0 to make the length of array to 4, the compiling errors disappear:
```systemverilog
    bti_demux #(
        .BTI_AW           (`RV_AW),
        .BTI_DW           (`RV_XLEN),
        .GST_SEL_AW       (BTI_GST_SEL_AW),
        .GST_NUM          (I_BTI_GST_NUM),
        .GST_SEL          ('{BOOT_ROM_SEL, ITCM_SEL, 8'h00, 8'h00}),
        .GST_AW           ('{BOOT_ROM_AW, ITCM_AW, 0, 0})
    ) // ...
```

This is the GitHub repo [link](https://github.com/fmj95817/rvbucket).
Running `./build.sh hw rtl verilator` can reproduce this issue.
Comment line 161(`build_sw_case boot "${1}"`) of `build.sh` before running.
